user_query:
I need a quick internal legal/compliance review for our Sri Lanka datasets before we circulate any microdata to collaborators. Please use the attached legal excerpts and dataset catalog to produce three things: a machine-generated compliance matrix and summary, structured meeting notes with action items, and a short email to external counsel with specific questions.

What to do and what to produce:
1) Implement and run a local script or command-line program (your choice) that reads the inputs under input/law and input/project and generates these artifacts:
   - output/compliance_matrix.csv with a header row and the following columns exactly: dataset_name, field_name, category, requires_explicit_consent, publication_risk, retention_risk, cross_border_risk, status, triggered_rules, rationale.
     • Use the rule IDs in the attached law excerpts to drive the analysis and populate triggered_rules (semicolon-separated IDs like PDPA-2;EA-1).
     • Set requires_explicit_consent = "Yes" for sensitive data per [PDPA-2]; otherwise "No".
     • Flag publication_risk with [EA-1], [EA-2], and/or [EA-3] when the dataset’s intended_publication and field content match the Elections Act excerpt.
     • Flag retention_risk = "Yes" under [PDPA-3] when retention_months > 24 and the dataset contains personal or sensitive data.
     • Flag cross_border_risk = "Yes" under [PDPA-4] when cross_border_transfer is true (note storage_country in the catalog) and add the rule ID.
     • Set status to Compliant, Needs review, or Non-compliant consistent with the rules (e.g., sensitive data without consent and no ethics approval should be Non-compliant under [PDPA-2]; any microdata that could reveal how an individual voted should be Non-compliant under [EA-1]; items that require safeguards or justification like cross-border transfers and >24 month retention can be Needs review).
     • Include a brief rationale for each flagged item that cites the relevant rule IDs by bracketed ID.
   - output/summary.json capturing top-level counts to support the meeting (keys and integer values): total_fields, sensitive_fields, fields_missing_consent, ea_publication_risks, retention_risks, cross_border_risks.

   The script must derive all of the above using only the provided inputs. Please keep logic grounded in the rule IDs and phrases included in the law excerpts.

2) Prepare output/meeting_notes.md that summarizes the findings for our internal compliance check-in. Include:
   - A summary section that references the numeric counts from output/summary.json (e.g., number of sensitive fields, how many have missing consent, etc.).
   - A Top risks section listing each dataset/field with Non-compliant or Needs review status and the precise rule IDs (e.g., [PDPA-2], [EA-1]).
   - An Action items section with owners and due-next steps, using these owner conventions: PI (consent and research-derogation decisions), Data Manager (retention and anonymization), Legal Counsel (publication and cross-border transfer constraints). At least one actionable item per risk.

3) Draft output/email_to_legal.txt addressed to our external counsel. It should:
   - Have a clear subject line about the Sri Lanka datasets compliance questions.
   - Summarize the top three legal/compliance questions arising from the analysis and reference the relevant rule IDs (e.g., [PDPA-4], [EA-2]).
   - Quote verbatim at least two bullet lines (including the bracketed ID) from the law excerpts in input/law to ground the questions. Please include the exact quoted lines as they appear in the files.
   - Refer to output/compliance_matrix.csv as the attachment we will share (just mention the path; do not actually attach anything).

Inputs to use (do not fetch anything from the web):
- input/law/pdpa_sl_excerpt.md
- input/law/elections_act_excerpt.md
- input/project/dataset_catalog.json

Expected outputs and paths:
- output/compliance_matrix.csv (CSV with the specified columns)
- output/summary.json (JSON with the specified keys)
- output/meeting_notes.md (Markdown)
- output/email_to_legal.txt (plain text)

Please ensure the script or CLI step is actually run to generate the CSV and JSON, and that the meeting notes and email are clearly informed by those outputs and the rule IDs from the provided law excerpts. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
